title: ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. godronii
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. godronii, also known as Godron's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to cause blistering and skin irritation when touched, which can be used to treat certain skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Godron's buttercup include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make ointments and creams, which are applied topically to affected areas for therapeutic purposes.
